blocks.text: Working Session Minutes: Stormwater Management Plan
Prepared by Pamela Warren, Project Engineer. Session held at the client's office. These minutes will be taken as accurate unless corrections are received at this office within ten days.

items: Pamela Warren, Project Engineer, Brackenridge Civil Group Inc
Charles Olson, Design Technician, Brackenridge Civil Group Inc
Jeffrey Perez, Design Technician, Brackenridge Civil Group Inc
David Dyer, General Manager, Johnson PLC

blocks.text: The session was called to fix the detention concept for {{fact:f:E-2004-001.client}} before the grading and drainage sheets are begun, and to resolve the outstanding information requests. The plan set has not been started and will not be started until the items under item 3 below are settled.

Charles Olson reported the results of the reconnaissance and of the re-shot outfall. The site divides between two discharge points rather than the single point shown on the survey, and the culvert beneath the access drive is silted and set lower than the survey gives it. Mr. Dyer asked whether the survey should be reordered. Charles Olson advised that it should not, on the ground that the differences are confined to the outfall and are now measured, and that a second survey would cost time we would not get back. This office concurs.

Two arrangements were presented. The first places a single basin at the low corner and requires a length of pipe to bring the second drainage area across the site to it. The second places two smaller basins, one at each discharge point, and avoids the pipe run at the cost of a second outlet structure to maintain. Mr. Dyer indicated a preference for the single basin on the ground that his operations staff would rather maintain one structure than two. Pamela Warren stated the condition attaching to that preference: the single basin works only if the pipe crossing can be held above the water table indicated in the geotechnical report, which this office has not yet seen. The concept is therefore preferred but not fixed, and will be confirmed in writing once the report is in hand.

Jeffrey Perez reported that the control sheets are drafted from the firm's standard details and that the limits of disturbance are the open item. He asked whether the contractor's laydown area would fall inside or outside those limits, as it governs where the perimeter measures run. Mr. Dyer stated that no contractor has been selected and that the question cannot be answered at this time. It was agreed that the sheets will show the limits at the improvements themselves and that a note will direct any additional disturbance to be enclosed by the contractor at his own cost.

The geotechnical report and the old grading permit remain outstanding, both requested at kickoff. Mr. Dyer stated that the property file is held at another office of Johnson PLC and undertook to have it sent. Pamela Warren stated that the detention concept cannot be fixed without the geotechnical report and that the schedule to submittal runs from the date it is received.

rows: Forward the geotechnical report and the property file to this office
Confirm the single-basin concept in writing on receipt of the geotechnical report
Re-shoot invert of the second discharge point and issue the field note to the file
Revise erosion control sheets to show limits at the improvements, with the contractor note added
Confirm the limits of the proposed improvements and notify this office of any change to the building footprint
Before grading is committed to sheets

blocks.text: The next working session will be held at this office. Pamela Warren will contact Mr. Dyer to arrange the date once the geotechnical report has been received.
